description: User authentication via Auth0 with MFA — verifies login, MFA prompt, session persistence, and logout flow
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the app root URL
Step 2: Verify redirect to Auth0 login page
Step 3: Enter valid email and password credentials
Step 4: Verify MFA prompt appears (TOTP or SMS)
Step 5: Enter a valid MFA code
Step 6: Verify redirect back to the app after successful authentication
Step 7: Verify the authenticated user's name or avatar appears in the UI
Step 8: Verify the app defaults to the Financial Dashboard of the last-active or default project
Step 9: Navigate to /api/auth/me and verify it returns the logged-in user's profile
Step 10: Click logout and verify session is destroyed
Step 11: Attempt to navigate to a protected route and verify redirect to login
Step 12: Attempt login with invalid credentials and verify an error message is shown

description: Project creation and management — verifies creating, editing, and viewing construction projects with all required fields
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the left sidebar and click the Project Dropdown Switcher
Step 2: Click 'New Project' to open the project creation flow
Step 3: Fill in project name, description, client name, client email, client phone, contract value, and contract number
Step 4: Set project start date and estimated end date
Step 5: Submit the form and verify the project appears in the dropdown switcher
Step 6: Select the newly created project and verify the app switches to its Financial Dashboard
Step 7: Navigate to Project & Schedule Builder and verify the project name is displayed
Step 8: Edit the project (change contract value) and save
Step 9: Verify the updated contract value is reflected in the Financial Dashboard's Total Contract Value card
Step 10: Call GET /api/projects/:projectId/summary and verify it returns contract value, status, phase count, and last activity
Step 11: Set project status to 'on_hold' and verify the status badge updates
Step 12: Delete the project and verify it no longer appears in the dropdown or portfolio view

description: Plaid bank account connection — linking a bank account, viewing connected accounts, and manual transaction sync
steps: Step 1: Navigate to Settings → Integrations
Step 2: Click 'Connect Bank Account' to initiate the Plaid Link modal
Step 3: Verify a Plaid sandbox connection modal appears in the app (dev stub — no real Plaid SDK required)
Step 4: Click 'Connect (Sandbox)' in the modal to simulate completing the Plaid Link flow and generate a mock public token
Step 5: Call POST /api/plaid/exchange-token with the public token and verify a bank_accounts record is created
Step 6: Navigate to Settings → Integrations and verify the newly connected bank appears in the connected accounts list with its last-synced timestamp
Step 7: Click 'Sync Now' next to the connected bank and verify a manual Plaid pull is triggered
Step 8: Call POST /api/bank-accounts/:bankAccountId/sync and verify the response confirms the sync job was queued
Step 9: Call GET /api/transactions?accountId=:bankAccountId and verify at least one transaction record is returned with matchStatus 'unmatched' (seeded by the sandbox sync)
Step 10: Click 'Remove' next to the connected account in Settings
Step 11: Verify the bank account is removed from the connected list

Step 12: Call DELETE /api/bank-accounts/:bankAccountId and verify the bank account record has isActive set to false; existing transactions are retained in the database (soft delete only, not hard-deleted)

description: Project Schedule Builder — phase accordion cards with expand/collapse and full phase CRUD operations
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the Project & Schedule Builder page
Step 2: Verify all existing phases are displayed as expanded accordion cards on first load
Step 3: Click a Phase Accordion Card header to collapse it and verify milestone rows are hidden
Step 4: Click the same header to expand it and verify milestone rows reappear
Step 5: Click 'Add Phase' and fill in phase name and description
Step 6: Save the phase and verify it appears as a new accordion card at the bottom
Step 7: Edit the phase name inline and verify the change persists on blur
Step 8: Verify each phase card shows a color-coded status badge (not_started, in_progress, completed)
Step 9: Verify each phase card shows a completion percentage computed from child milestones
Step 10: Delete a phase and verify all child milestones are removed
Step 11: Call GET /api/projects/:projectId/phases and verify ordered phase list with completion percentages

description: Milestone management — adding, editing, reordering within a phase, and cross-phase drag-and-drop reassignment
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the Project & Schedule Builder page
Step 2: Click the plus button (top-right) to open the Add Milestone modal
Step 3: Fill in milestone title, scheduled dollar amount, phase assignment, trigger definition, and at least one completion criterion
Step 4: Click 'Add Milestone' and verify it appears as a new row inside the selected phase
Step 5: Verify the milestone row shows title, scheduled amount, and a Status Badge (gray = Draft)
Step 6: Click the milestone title inline to edit it and press Enter to save
Step 7: Click the dollar value inline to edit it and press Tab to save
Step 8: Grab the texture-dot grip handle of a milestone and drag it upward within the same phase to reorder
Step 9: Verify the new sort_order persists after a page refresh (call GET /api/phases/:phaseId/milestones)
Step 10: Drag a milestone across a phase boundary and verify it now belongs to the destination phase
Step 11: Call PATCH /api/milestones/reorder with cross-phase payload and verify phaseId and sortOrder updated
Step 12: Delete a milestone via the row action menu and verify it is removed from the UI
Step 13: Verify the parent phase completion percentage updates after milestone deletion

description: Ready-to-Invoice toggle and QuickBooks invoice generation confirmation modal
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the Project & Schedule Builder and locate a milestone with status 'criteria_met' or 'approved'
Step 2: Flip the 'Ready to Invoice' slide-toggle to the green (on) position
Step 3: Verify a confirmation modal immediately appears
Step 4: Verify the modal displays the projected QuickBooks invoice number and the milestone's scheduled dollar amount
Step 5: Click 'Cancel' inside the modal and verify the toggle reverts to its original off state
Step 6: Flip the toggle again and this time click 'Proceed'
Step 7: Verify the milestone status badge updates to 'Ready to Invoice' (blue badge)
Step 8: Verify the ready_to_invoice field is true when calling GET /api/milestones/:milestoneId
Step 9: Verify ready_to_invoice_triggered_at and ready_to_invoice_triggered_by are populated
Step 10: Verify clicking outside the modal (when open) does NOT dismiss it (accidental prevention guard)
Step 11: Call PATCH /api/milestones/:milestoneId/ready-to-invoice and verify the response includes projected invoice number and dollar amount
Step 12: Verify the Dynamic Action-Item Banner on the Financial Dashboard no longer flags this milestone as un-invoiced

description: Milestone criteria management — adding criteria, marking as met, and auto-triggering status progression
steps: Step 1: Open the Add Milestone modal and add three completion criteria for a new milestone
Step 2: Save the milestone and verify all three criteria are visible in the milestone's expanded row
Step 3: Click one criterion's checkbox to mark it as met
Step 4: Verify is_met is true and met_at is populated via GET /api/milestones/:milestoneId/criteria
Step 5: Mark all remaining criteria as met
Step 6: Verify the milestone status automatically progresses to 'criteria_met'
Step 7: Verify the status badge color updates accordingly
Step 8: Add a new criterion to an existing milestone via POST /api/milestones/:milestoneId/criteria
Step 9: Delete a criterion and verify the remaining criteria count is correct

description: Document attachment vault — uploading inspection PDFs and site photos to milestones and viewing them
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the Project & Schedule Builder and locate a milestone row
Step 2: Click the Inline Media Dock thumbnail slot at the end of the milestone row
Step 3: Upload a PDF file (simulating an inspection report)
Step 4: Verify the PDF appears as a thumbnail preview in the Inline Media Dock
Step 5: Upload a JPG image file (simulating a site photo)
Step 6: Verify both the PDF and JPG thumbnails are visible in the dock
Step 7: Click a thumbnail to open the full document viewer
Step 8: Call GET /api/milestones/:milestoneId/documents and verify both documents are returned with correct file_type, category, and file_url
Step 9: Delete one document via DELETE /api/milestones/:milestoneId/documents/:documentId and verify it disappears from the dock

description: Financial Dashboard — four metric cards display correct real-time values and update on transaction/milestone changes
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the Financial Dashboard of a project with at least one invoiced and one paid milestone
Step 2: Verify the 'Total Contract Value' card shows the project's contract_value field
Step 3: Verify the 'Invoiced to Date' card shows the sum of scheduled amounts for milestones in invoiced/paid status
Step 4: Verify the 'Cash Collected' card shows the sum of matched transaction amounts for paid milestones
Step 5: Verify the 'Unbilled Progress' card shows the value of approved milestones not yet invoiced
Step 6: Navigate to the Bank Sync page and confirm a new transaction match for a milestone
Step 7: Return to the Financial Dashboard and verify the 'Cash Collected' card has updated in real-time (or near real-time)
Step 8: Verify the gross profit margin display updates accordingly
Step 9: Call GET /api/projects/:projectId/dashboard and verify all four metric values match the UI
Step 10: Toggle to 'Portfolio View' and verify aggregate totals across all active projects are shown
Step 11: Call GET /api/dashboard/portfolio and verify it returns combined metric cards
Step 12: Verify metric cards display formatted currency (e.g., $125,000 not 125000)

description: Cash Flow Burn-Down Chart — dual-line interactive chart with date range selector and tooltip on hover
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the Financial Dashboard and locate the Cash Flow Burn-Down Chart
Step 2: Verify the chart renders two lines: green (cumulative income) and red (cumulative expenses)
Step 3: Hover over a data point on the green income line and verify a tooltip appears with the exact dollar total and date
Step 4: Hover over a data point on the red expense line and verify the tooltip shows the cumulative expense total
Step 5: Use the date range selector to switch to 'Monthly' view
Step 6: Verify the chart x-axis updates to show monthly intervals
Step 7: Switch to 'Quarterly' view and verify chart updates
Step 8: Switch to 'Project Lifetime' view and verify chart covers the full project date range
Step 9: Call GET /api/projects/:projectId/snapshots?interval=month and verify time-series data matches chart data points
Step 10: Add a new expense and verify the red line updates on the chart after refresh
Step 11: Verify the chart is interactive and zoomable or pannable on desktop
Step 12: Verify the chart renders without errors when there is no data (empty state message)

description: Dynamic Action-Item Banner and financial alerts — proactive notifications for un-invoiced milestones and overdue draws
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the Financial Dashboard with at least one approved-but-uninvoiced milestone
Step 2: Verify the Dynamic Action-Item Banner appears below the metric cards
Step 3: Verify the banner message references the total dollar value and count of ready-to-invoice milestones
Step 4: Click the banner CTA and verify navigation jumps to the Schedule Builder with the relevant milestone highlighted
Step 5: Click 'Dismiss' on an individual alert in the banner
Step 6: Verify the dismissed alert disappears from the banner
Step 7: Call PATCH /api/alerts/:alertId/dismiss and verify is_dismissed becomes true
Step 8: Refresh the page and verify dismissed alerts do not reappear
Step 9: Call GET /api/projects/:projectId/alerts and verify alert_type, severity, and message fields
Step 10: Create a scenario where an expense pushes a phase over budget and verify a 'budget_overrun' alert appears
Step 11: Verify critical severity alerts have distinct visual styling (e.g., red border or icon)
Step 12: Verify info severity alerts have a neutral/blue styling distinct from warnings

description: Financial report export — generating and downloading PDF and CSV reports for lenders, clients, and accountants
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the Financial Dashboard and click 'Export Report'
Step 2: Verify a report configuration dialog appears with options for report type, date range, output format, and recipient label
Step 3: Select report type 'cash_flow', output format 'PDF', and recipient label 'Lender'
Step 4: Set a custom date range (e.g., last 3 months)
Step 5: Click 'Generate Report' and verify a loading indicator appears
Step 6: Verify the PDF file downloads automatically once generated
Step 7: Open the downloaded PDF and verify it contains the project name, date range, and financial data
Step 8: Repeat the process selecting 'CSV' format and recipient label 'Accountant'
Step 9: Verify the CSV file downloads and contains column headers and data rows
Step 10: Call POST /api/projects/:projectId/reports with the report payload and verify a report record is returned with a file_url
Step 11: Call GET /api/reports/:reportId/download and verify the file stream is returned
Step 12: Generate a portfolio-level report (project_id = null) and verify it aggregates data across all active projects

description: Transaction ledger and AI-suggested matching — unmatched bank feed, transaction selection, and ranked milestone candidates
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the Bank Sync Transaction Matcher page
Step 2: Verify the Left Split-Pane shows a vertical feed of cleared deposits sorted by posted_date descending
Step 3: Verify each transaction row shows date, raw bank memo, check image thumbnail (if available), and bold deposit amount
Step 4: Click a transaction row to select it
Step 5: Verify the Right Split-Pane updates immediately to show AI-suggested milestone matches
Step 6: Verify each suggested match shows a Match Score Badge (percentage) with rationale (amount match, date proximity, OCR keyword overlap)
Step 7: Verify matches are ranked by confidence score descending
Step 8: Call GET /api/transactions/:transactionId/suggested-matches and verify the ranked list with match_confidence_score values
Step 9: Click 'Ignore' on a transaction row and verify it disappears from the unmatched feed
Step 10: Call PATCH /api/transactions/:transactionId/ignore and verify match_status is 'ignored'
Step 11: Filter the transaction feed by status='unmatched' and verify only unmatched transactions appear
Step 12: Filter by date range and verify the feed is constrained to that range
Step 13: Click a check image thumbnail to open the Transaction Detail Modal

description: Link & Sync reconciliation — confirming a match, QuickBooks write-back, milestone status update, and undo match
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the Bank Sync page and select an unmatched transaction
Step 2: Select a suggested milestone match in the Right Split-Pane
Step 3: Click 'Link & Sync' to confirm the match
Step 4: Verify the transaction row animates (slides and locks onto the milestone visual)
Step 5: Verify the transaction's match_status changes to 'manually_matched' or 'auto_matched'
Step 6: Verify the matched milestone's status updates to 'paid'
Step 7: Call POST /api/transactions/:transactionId/match and verify the response contains milestone status and QuickBooks sync fields
Step 8: Verify a quickbooks_invoice_id and quickbooks_payment_id are generated in the transaction_matches record
Step 9: Call GET /api/integrations/quickbooks/status and verify the connection shows 'connected' and the write-back is queued or synced
Step 10: Click 'Undo Match' on the reconciled transaction
Step 11: Verify the transaction reverts to unmatched status and the milestone reverts to its previous status
Step 12: Call DELETE /api/transactions/:transactionId/match and verify the match record is removed
Step 13: Verify the undo action is logged in the reconciliation_audit_log with action 'match_undone'

description: Split-payment utility drawer — allocating a single transaction across multiple milestones with running balance
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the Bank Sync page and select a large deposit that covers multiple milestone amounts
Step 2: Click 'Split Payment' on the transaction row
Step 3: Verify the Split-Payment Utility Drawer slides in from the right edge of the screen
Step 4: Verify the drawer shows the total transaction amount at the top as the starting balance
Step 5: Select the first milestone from the list and enter an allocated amount
Step 6: Verify the running balance decrements by the allocated amount in real-time
Step 7: Select a second milestone and enter its allocated amount
Step 8: Verify the running balance continues to decrement toward $0
Step 9: Attempt to enter an allocation that exceeds the remaining balance and verify an error is shown
Step 10: Complete allocations so the running balance reaches exactly $0
Step 11: Click 'Confirm Split' and verify all selected milestones update to 'paid' status
Step 12: Call POST /api/transactions/:transactionId/split with the allocations payload and verify split_payment_allocation and split_payment_allocation_lines records are created
Step 13: Close the drawer without confirming and verify all unsaved allocations are discarded
Step 14: Call PUT /api/transactions/:transactionId/split to update an existing split and verify amounts are adjusted

description: Reconciliation audit trail — viewing full history of match actions, confirmations, splits, and QuickBooks events
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the Bank Sync Transaction Matcher page
Step 2: Click 'View Audit Log' at the bottom of the page
Step 3: Verify the full historical reconciliation trail is displayed
Step 4: Verify each audit entry shows actor identity (user name), timestamp, and action type
Step 5: Verify action types include: auto_match_proposed, manually_matched, match_undone, split_created, quickbooks_synced
Step 6: Click on a specific transaction to open its scoped audit trail
Step 7: Verify the transaction-scoped log shows only entries related to that transaction
Step 8: Call GET /api/reconciliation/audit-log?projectId=X and verify paginated results
Step 9: Call GET /api/transactions/:transactionId/audit-log and verify scoped entries with previous_state and new_state JSON fields

description: Global search bar — searching across check numbers, project names, and client names in real-time
steps: Step 1: Click the global search bar in the top header
Step 2: Type a partial project name and verify real-time suggestions appear below the search bar
Step 3: Click a project result and verify the app navigates to that project's dashboard
Step 4: Open the search bar again and type a client name
Step 5: Verify results include projects matching the client name
Step 6: Type a check number and verify the matching transaction appears in results
Step 7: Click the check number result and verify navigation to the Transaction Detail Modal
Step 8: Call GET /api/search?q=test&type=project and verify the response structure
Step 9: Verify the search bar clears and closes on Escape keypress

description: QuickBooks integration — OAuth connection, sync indicator badge, item code assignment, and Force Sync
steps: Step 1: Navigate to Settings → Integrations
Step 2: Verify QuickBooks connection status is shown (connected/needs-reauth/disconnected)
Step 3: Click 'Connect QuickBooks' to initiate the OAuth flow
Step 4: Simulate OAuth redirect and callback with a valid authorization code
Step 5: Verify the top-header QuickBooks Sync Indicator badge turns green after successful connection
Step 6: Navigate to the Project & Schedule Builder and open a milestone
Step 7: Click the QuickBooks item code selector on the milestone
Step 8: Verify available QB item codes are fetched from GET /api/integrations/quickbooks/item-codes
Step 9: Select an item code and verify it is saved to the milestone's quickbooks_item_code field
Step 10: Simulate a failed sync (set quickbooks_sync_status to 'failed' on a transaction_match) and verify the top-header badge turns amber
Step 11: Click the amber badge and verify it triggers re-authentication without navigating away
Step 12: Navigate to Settings → Integrations and click 'Force Sync'
Step 13: Call POST /api/integrations/quickbooks/sync and verify pending/failed records are queued for write-back

description: Design system consistency — verifies color palette, typography, button variants, card styling, and animation timings across all pages
steps: Step 1: Navigate to each main page (Schedule Builder, Financial Dashboard, Bank Sync, Settings)
Step 2: Verify the primary accent color (#CC785C orange/amber) is used for primary action buttons and active states
Step 3: Verify background color is white in light mode and #1A1A1A in dark mode
Step 4: Verify headings use font-semibold and body text uses font-normal with leading-relaxed
Step 5: Verify card components have rounded corners (8px), subtle border or shadow, and p-4 to p-6 padding
Step 6: Verify hover states on cards show a slight shadow increase
Step 7: Verify all transitions and animations occur between 150ms and 300ms
Step 8: Toggle dark mode and verify all surfaces, text colors, and borders follow the dark palette from the spec
Step 9: Verify primary buttons have orange/amber background with white text and rounded corners
Step 10: Verify secondary buttons use a border style with hover fill

description: Responsive layout and mobile accessibility — verifies mobile-first design, collapsible sidebar, touch targets, and PWA support
steps: Step 1: Open the app on a desktop viewport (1280px+) and verify the left sidebar is visible by default
Step 2: Click the collapse arrow on the sidebar and verify it hides and the main content expands to fill the space
Step 3: Click again and verify the sidebar restores
Step 4: Resize the browser to a mobile viewport (375px width)
Step 5: Verify the sidebar is collapsed by default on mobile
Step 6: Tap the hamburger/menu icon to open the sidebar and verify it overlays the content
Step 7: Verify swipe gesture (swipe right) opens the sidebar and swipe left closes it
Step 8: Verify all interactive elements (buttons, toggles, milestone rows) have touch targets of at least 44x44px
Step 9: Verify the Financial Dashboard metric cards stack vertically on mobile
Step 10: Verify the Bank Sync split pane switches to a tabbed or stacked layout on mobile
Step 11: Verify the app manifest and service worker are present for PWA support
Step 12: Verify the app can be installed as a PWA on Chrome mobile without errors

description: Accessibility compliance — keyboard navigation, screen reader support, ARIA labels, high contrast mode, and reduced motion
steps: Step 1: Tab through the entire app navigation using only the keyboard and verify every interactive element receives focus in logical order
Step 2: Verify all buttons, toggles, and links have visible focus rings
Step 3: Use a screen reader (VoiceOver or NVDA) and navigate to the Financial Dashboard — verify metric cards are announced with their label and value
Step 4: Navigate to the Schedule Builder and verify milestone rows, status badges, and toggles are correctly announced by the screen reader
Step 5: Verify all form inputs have associated <label> elements or aria-label attributes
Step 6: Verify all icons that convey meaning have aria-label or visually hidden text
Step 7: Verify modal dialogs trap focus while open and restore focus to the trigger element on close
Step 8: Enable high contrast mode in the OS and verify the app remains fully legible with no invisible text or icons
Step 9: Enable 'Reduce Motion' in the OS accessibility settings and verify all CSS transitions/animations are disabled or simplified
Step 10: Run an automated accessibility audit (e.g., axe-core) on the main pages and verify zero critical violations
